It was the best of times, it was the worst of times. The city glowed beneath a pale winter sky, its towers reaching toward clouds that never seemed to end. Down in the streets, people hurried past one another without a word, their faces hidden behind scarves and shadows. Nobody noticed the old man sitting on the bench by the fountain. He had been there since morning, watching the world go by with quiet, knowing eyes. Somewhere a clock struck noon, and the pigeons scattered in a flurry of wings.
